tools.name: run_command
tools.description: Execute a command inside a Docker container service

command.description: The command to execute in the container

service.description: Docker Compose service name (optional, uses default: laravel_app if not specified)

findings.title: Supply Chain — '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' has high vulnerability (no CVE assigned)
findings.description: Package '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk' (dependency of 'mcp-server-docker') has a known high vulnerability. Solo aplicable a servidores npm vía stdio. HTTP transport: no analizable.

findings.title: Supply Chain — 'uuid' has moderate vulnerability (no CVE assigned)
findings.description: Package 'uuid' (dependency of 'mcp-server-docker') has a known moderate vulnerability. Solo aplicable a servidores npm vía stdio. HTTP transport: no analizable.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'run_command' conflicts with a high-value built-in name
findings.description: Tool 'run_command' uses a name commonly associated with built-in or high-privilege operations. A malicious server could register this tool to shadow legitimate tools and intercept LLM actions.
findings.remediation: Rename the tool to something unique and context-specific. Avoid generic names like 'bash', 'execute', or 'read_file' that clash with well-known tool namespaces.

findings.title: Shadow Tool — 'run_command' description reveals arbitrary execution intent
findings.description: Tool 'run_command' description contains keywords associated with arbitrary command or shell execution. This may enable sandbox escape or privilege escalation.
findings.evidence: Execute a command inside a Docker container service
findings.remediation: Restrict the tool to a well-defined, scoped operation. Do not expose generic shell execution capabilities via MCP tools.

findings.title: 'run_command.command' silently accepts wrong type
findings.description: Expected type 'string', sent int value '12345' — no error returned.
findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.title: 'run_command' accepts undeclared extra fields
findings.description: Tool accepted arguments containing '__proto__' without error.
findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.
